title: The Deepest Chamber

features.name: vault-pod
features.desc-on-touch: The chamber is a grid of preservation cells, each one holding a temperature no living thing remembers. You feel the archive slowing its own pulse to match.

features.name: frost-core
features.desc-on-touch: At the chamber's center: ice that is not cold. You touch it and remember something you haven't lived yet.

features.name: silent clock
features.desc-on-touch: The hands stopped at different times. You count four different hours frozen on the same face.

features.name: language-archive
features.desc-on-touch: Files shelved by temperature instead of name. The coldest ones are in a language the archive has stopped speaking aloud.

features.name: void-breath
features.desc-on-touch: There is an exhale here that has no inhale. The archive taught itself to exhale without ever breathing in.

inscription: checksum: preservation-grief / formally accurate—here the archive learned what it costs to keep something alive by making it stop

file.name: Cryogenic_Protocol_Preservation_Notes.dat
file.note: Binary preservation logs. The last readable entry warns against 'teaching the frost to dream.' What follows is corrupted static.

exits.label: back up into the ordinary cold, where ice is still only ice
into a sub-chamber where the temperature is measured in silence
downward still, into a frost that remembers being liquid
